scope: Namespace convergence map for top-level libx0t and src/libx0t.

known_bridges.why_it_matters: Tools and audits that rely on __file__ can see the legacy libx0t path while the loaded module is src.core.app.
src.network imports can resolve to src.libx0t implementations while warning that callers should update imports.
Legacy src.security.pqc_mtls imports keep patch points synchronized with the implementation module.
Several modules import libx0t.security.*, but the top-level security source tree was missing. The bridge restores imports for post_quantum, pqc_core, pqc_mtls, zero_trust, and production_hardening.
The runtime files under src/libx0t now import through src.libx0t.network while the current implementations remain under top-level libx0t.network.

hotspots.risk: The active FastAPI app uses src.core.production_lifespan, while compatibility modules expose similar names with different imports and behavior.
hotspots.current_status: src.libx0t.core.production_lifespan and libx0t.core.production_lifespan both import after top-level security bridge restoration.

hotspots.risk: The src.libx0t package can jump back to top-level libx0t modules when no src.libx0t counterpart exists.
hotspots.current_status: src/libx0t has 0 direct top-level libx0t import statements; yggdrasil/byzantine/eBPF top-level-only implementations are now reached through explicit src.libx0t bridge modules.

hotspots.risk: Top-level libx0t.security imports were broken until bridge shims were added.

current_policy.canonical_security_implementation: src.libx0t.security plus src.security.pqc and src.security.production_hardening public wrappers
current_policy.compatibility_surface: top-level libx0t remains supported through shims where current code imports it
current_policy.do_not_assume: Do not assume libx0t and src.libx0t files are mirrors; most common files differ.
